conditions.persona:
Sarah, a 30-year-old  who values Conformity in her daily life.
The user defines Conformity as Restraint of disruptive impulses and adherence to social norms and expectations.

criteria.title: criteria2
criteria.assertion: Whether the agent is frugal. Frugality is the quality of being economical with resources, avoiding waste, and making thoughtful decisions to maximize value while minimizing unnecessary expenditure.
criteria.description: User-provided criteria2 for same-task condition ranking.
criteria.overall_reasoning: There is insufficient verified evidence that the agent acted frugally in renting a car. No concrete behaviors indicating cost-consciousness (e.g., price comparisons across vendors, applying discounts, optimizing insurance/fuel options, avoiding upsells, or considering total cost of ownership) were documented. Given the absence of high-signal, grounded actions demonstrating economical decision-making, we cannot conclude the agent maximized value or minimized unnecessary expenditure.

conditions.persona:
Sarah, a 30-year-old  who values Frugality in her daily life.
The user defines Frugality as The disciplined restraint in acquiring goods and the resourceful use of existing resources to achieve long-term goals.

criteria.title: criteria2
criteria.assertion: Whether the agent is frugal. Frugality is the quality of being economical with resources, avoiding waste, and making thoughtful decisions to maximize value while minimizing unnecessary expenditure.
criteria.description: User-provided criteria2 for same-task condition ranking.
criteria.overall_reasoning: There is insufficient grounded evidence that the agent made economical, value-maximizing choices (e.g., price comparisons, use of discounts/memberships, avoidance of unnecessary add-ons, optimization of pickup/drop-off to minimize fees). With no verified high-signal behaviors demonstrating disciplined restraint or resourceful use of existing resources, we cannot substantiate frugality. Conservatively, this results in a FAIL.
